name: pypicloud
perf_notes: No published benchmarks. v1.3.9 added file streaming to lower memory usage on large package uploads/downloads (previously buffered in memory). It is a synchronous WSGI Pyramid app, typically served by waitress/gunicorn behind a reverse proxy; throughput depends on the chosen WSGI server and the cache backend (SQL/DynamoDB/Redis). LDAP backend uses aggressive caching to limit auth-server load.
scale_notes: Designed for cloud object storage (S3/GCS/Azure) as the source of truth plus a separate metadata cache layer (SQL, DynamoDB, or Redis incl. Redis Cluster). Because storage and cache are external/shared, multiple stateless pypicloud instances can run concurrently behind a load balancer for horizontal scaling and HA. No built-in clustering/leader election; multi-node coordination relies entirely on the shared S3+DynamoDB/Redis backends. The cache DB is a regenerable view that can be rebuilt from storage.
summary: A Pyramid-based private/proxying PyPI server that stores packages on cloud object storage (S3, GCS, or Azure Blob) with a pluggable metadata cache (SQL/DynamoDB/Redis); now in maintenance mode and archived.
extras: Three-layer pluggable architecture: storage backend (filesystem/S3/GCS/Azure) + cache backend (SQL/DynamoDB/Redis) + Pyramid web app
pypi.fallback setting controls upstream behavior: redirect (302 to upstream, default), cache (download & store locally), or none (404)
pypi.always_show_upstream blends local and upstream versions in one index
Five access-control backends: config-file, SQL (web-admin), LDAP/AD, AWS Secrets Manager, and a remote/delegated HTTP backend
Per-package read/write permissions for users and groups, with automatic 'everyone' and 'authenticated' groups and admin role
Auto-computes SHA256/MD5 package hashes on upload (pypi.calculate_package_hashes)
Configurable overwrite and delete policies via allow_overwrite_groups / allow_delete_groups
Built-in web UI for browsing, uploading, and managing packages
Supports pip search via an XML-RPC endpoint (/pypi)
Redis Cluster support for the cache layer (added in 1.3.8)
Official companion Docker image at github.com/stevearc/pypicloud-docker (config templated from env vars)
Project is archived and in maintenance mode as of 2023; only bug fixes were accepted before archival
